Covers the _on_session_exit HANDOFF_PENDING fix, spawn_new_child callback restore, and cli.py continue-vs-break path.

title: Worker-token spike — measure per-worker context tokens separately from orchestrator
description: Instrument worker agent sessions to capture token consumption independently of the orchestrator session. Goal: build a two-component capacity model (orchestrator overhead + worker execution) to replace the current single EWMA that conflates both. Measure 3+ orchestrate rounds, compute mean worker-session tokens, update calibration JSON. Expected finding: worker tokens ~50% of current EWMA, enabling more rounds per 5hr window.

title: Proxy SSE response parser — capture output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens per request
description: Parse the upstream SSE response in agentflow/proxy/server.py to extract usage fields from the message_start event: output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens. Add these to the _log_entry() call so proxy_log.jsonl contains a complete per-request cost breakdown (input compression + cache hits + output tokens). Handle both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming (JSON body) responses.

title: HANDOFF RECOMMENDED as PTY stall-recovery trigger — reconcile _task_start_tokens against tasks.json
description: output_handler.py: when HANDOFF RECOMMENDED detected in PTY output, cross-check _task_start_tokens against tasks.json and evict any task already marked complete. Then re-evaluate threshold gate. Recovers from cases where PostToolUse hook updated tasks.json but AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE was never emitted in PTY stream.

title: Deterministic session_type via hook — write session_state.json on /orchestrate or /oracle prompt; PTY reads instead of scanning output
description: UserPromptSubmit hook writes {"session_type": "orchestrator"|"oracle"} to .agentflow/session_state.json when /orchestrate or /oracle detected in prompt. PTY session_manager reads this file at startup and after restart injection (write_input path also sets it directly). Removes race condition where trigger_handoff fires before /orchestrate echoes back; eliminates false positives from /orchestrate in code block output.

Use tmp_path for ~/.claude/settings.json reads/writes. Mock os.replace with a real temp-file approach.

Extract the compression/logging logic into agentflow/proxy/compress.py. server.py becomes the thin HTTP server only (<= 150 lines). compress.py handles compress(), _log_proxy_entry(), and related helpers.

title: Orchestrate startup cache — pre-compute current_round + slim pending into .agentflow/orchestrate_cache.json
description: Write agentflow/shell/orchestrate_cache.py that generates .agentflow/orchestrate_cache.json from tasks.json + execution_plan.md + rate_calibration. Cache contains: current_round name, pending tasks for that round (id+title+deps only), all_pending count, unresolved_design_decisions count, ewma_mean_tokens, ewma_cv, sample_count. Regenerate only when source file mtimes change. Update startup.md to read cache file first (mtime check), falling back to full startup if stale. Saves 4 bash commands + 1 IDX read from orchestrator context on every startup.

title: Per-task token estimator — regression model from task_token_log.jsonl
description: Build empirical estimator to replace the 2500-token constant in round-sizing. (1) New file agentflow/shadow/task_estimator.py: reads ~/.agentflow/task_token_log.jsonl. (2) Requires sample_count >= 5; else returns fallback constant 2500. (3) Fits linear regression: tokens ~ intercept + slope_lines * estimated_lines + slope_files * file_count using least squares (stdlib only — no numpy; implement normal equations directly). (4) Writes model to ~/.agentflow/task_estimator.json: {slope_lines, slope_files, intercept, r2, sample_count, updated_at}. (5) Exposes estimate(estimated_lines, file_count) -> int. (6) Tests: test_task_estimator.py — synthetic log entries; assert model written; assert fallback on low sample count; assert estimate() output reasonable.

title: Wire calibrate_capacity() at handoff + add ewma_cv computation
description: Call calibrate_capacity() automatically at each handoff using ledger start/end pct (from T-163). Add ewma_cv computation to capacity_calibrator.py: track per-session pct_per_task values, compute std/mean CV across sessions, write ewma_cv + sample_count to rate_calibration_claude.json. Oracle already reads ewma_cv for task sizing — this closes the gap. Files: agentflow/shadow/capacity_calibrator.py, agentflow/shell/session_manager.py (handoff hook).

title: Split agentflow/shell/session_manager.py — size violation
description: Split agentflow/shell/session_manager.py (252 lines, limit 250) by domain — NOT by line count. Two domains: (1) state machine coordination: poll(), on_idle_tick(), on_enter_*(), trigger_handoff(), _check_drain_restart(), _apply_session_threshold(), _sync_session_type() — stays in session_manager.py; (2) child process I/O: _spawn_new_child(), restart_child(), _handle_output(), _ansi_strip(), _detect_read_path(), _record_task_tokens(), _on_session_exit() — extract to session_io.py. session_manager.py imports from session_io. title: Orchestrate drain restart — direct RESTARTING path, no output parsing
description:
Remove all output-based PTY restart triggers and replace with a deterministic file-based drain path for orchestrate sessions.

## Changes

1. **Add direct RESTARTING transition for orchestrate drain path** (`handoff_handler.check_drain_restart`): instead of calling `trigger_handoff()` (which routes through HANDOFF_PENDING and waits for HANDOFF_COMPLETE), transition directly to RESTARTING when conditions are met (tasks_in_flight=[] AND context_fill>=80K AND on_idle_tick). HANDOFF_PENDING remains intact for oracle sessions — orchestrate gets its own transition only.

2. **Call `clear_signal_files()` before `execvp`** in `process_manager.handle_enter_restarting` so the new session starts with context_fill reset to 0. Ensures no duplicate restart on session start.

3. **Delete output-based triggers** in `output_handler.py`: line 84 (HANDOFF_COMPLETE text detection) and lines 126-162 (HANDOFF RECOMMENDED + accumulated-token trigger). These are the fragile output-parsing paths.

4. **Delete `reset_accumulator`**: remove the `reset_file.touch()` call in `user_prompt_submit.py:184` and delete `.agentflow/reset_accumulator` from the repo. Nothing in the PTY shell reads this file — it is a dead artifact.

5. **Rename/comment `_last_accumulated_tokens`** to make clear it is observability-only (audit logs + TASK_COMPLETE state transition for oracle). Add a comment: 'output token accumulator — observability only, not used for restart decisions'.

## Guards that make restart deterministic
- Guard 1: tasks_in_flight.json must be [] tombstone (tasks done)
- Guard 2: check_drain_restart only runs in on_idle_tick (Claude idle between turns)
- Both must be true simultaneously — no false-positive restart possible

## Files
- agentflow/shell/handoff_handler.py (drain path transition)
- agentflow/shell/process_manager.py (clear_signal_files before execvp)
- agentflow/shell/output_handler.py (delete output-based triggers)
- agentflow/shell/session_manager.py (comment on _last_accumulated_tokens)
- agentflow/hooks/user_prompt_submit.py (remove reset_accumulator write)
- .agentflow/reset_accumulator (delete)

## Estimated lines changed: ~60
## Model: Sonnet

title: Fix check_drain_restart: no_current_round blocks restart after merge detection (T-269 regression)
description: check_drain_restart gates on current_round.json existing as its first check.
